revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Jinbayama Kofun]][[ja:仁馬山古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name = Jinbayama Kofun | native_name =仁馬山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Jinbayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.013853|N|130.942203|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|qid=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|0|49.87|N|130|56|31.93|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Jinbayama Kofun'''|仁馬山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in the Nobuyuki neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi|Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi]] in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|qid=Q398087|lt=San'yō region|1=San'yō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1991.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162258|title= 仁馬山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the largest Kofun in the {{ill|Nagato Province|en|Nagato Province|qid=Q907382|lt=Nagato region|1=Nagato Province}} (western Yamaguchi Prefecture) and the third largest in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and is estimated to have been built in the latter half of the 4th century (the first half of the Kofun period).
==Overview== The Jinbayama Kofun is located on the eastern edge of a plateau near the mouth of the Ayaragi Plain in the westernmost part of Yamaguchi Prefecture. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It was discovered in 1902 and was reported at the time to be a "gourd-shaped" tumulus, as the anterior portion of the mound is relatively undeveloped. Only the posterior circular portion was constructed in three stages, with a diameter of 47 meters and height of eight meters. The anterior portion has a width of 26 meters. From fragments of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' is presumed to have had rows of cylindrical ''haniwa'' on each stage; however, ''[[fukiishi]]'' have not been found. The total length of the tumulus is approximately 75-meters and it is orientated to the west-northwest. According to the [[archaeological excavation]] conducted from 2005 to 2008, the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was found to contain a clay sarcophagus encasing a wooden split bamboo-shaped coffin. The coffin is unusually long, measuring about one meter in diameter by 6.2 meters in length. As the burial chamber was intact, it is believed to contain contemporary {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} in good preservation, but the excavation concluded before the sarcophagus could be opened.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=43dd750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In addition, two small round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style ''kofun'' (Jinbayama north tumulus and Jinbayama south tumulus), which are thought to be ''baizuka'' associated mounds, have been identified at the southern edge of the main tumulus.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
At present, the trees around the tumulus have been cut down, and it can be seen from the plains to the south. The tumulus is about 30 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kajikuri-Gōdaichi Station|en|Kajikuri-Gōdaichi Station|qid=Q6348998|lt=Kajikuri-Gōdaichi Station|1=Kajikuri-Gōdaichi Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|qid=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamaguchi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamaguchi)|qid=Q6573292|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamaguchi)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamaguchi)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.shimo-kouko.jp/exhibition/shisekinomichi/#jinmayama Shimonoseki Archaeological Museum home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://bunkazai.pref.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=dd0052 Yamaguchi Prefecture Tourism, Sports and Culture Department Culture Promotion Division]{{in lang|ja}}
